packageName: First Nations Governance Commons Public Demo
recommendedDecision: Proceed with private controlled pilot preparation using fake/sample data only. Keep real participant intake and live Decidim access blocked until every critical release lock, execution gate, support route, access control, acceptance test, rollback path, and public communication passes privately.

localDecidimStatus.githubCliAuth: hold: logged in as Shadow-3 without project scope
localDecidimStatus.node: hold: v24.14.0 detected; Decidim develop docs currently list Node 22.14.x for manual setup
localDecidimStatus.browserTestTooling: hold: Chromium/Playwright available through Codex tooling, not confirmed as project dependency
localDecidimStatus.decidimApp: blocked: no real Decidim app scaffold exists in this static package

safetyRules: Fake/sample data only in public demo and sandbox.
No real community records.
No member lists.
No cultural material.
No real voting or eligibility data.
Real pilot requires separate governance, data, privacy, cultural safety, and hosting approvals.
No youth records or guardian/school/program records.
No personal service cases or complaints.
No real provider contract or spend approval.
No real milestone payment release or procurement decision.
No real membership applications or Toastmasters member numbers.
No payment, address, phone, email, access-need, signature, official club record, private organisation contact, or real authority data in the public demo.
Toastmasters First Nations is a controlled sample only; real intake, meeting links, official paperwork, sponsorship/payment reconciliation, and charter acceptance stay outside the package.
No real private pilot records in the public demo, public repository, public screenshots, or fake-data sandbox.
Private pilot cannot start until privacy notice, consent, role access, data-transfer map, incident response, hosting, withdrawal/deletion, official handoff, and closeout are approved.
Organisation supporters cannot receive member data, access needs, payments, official acceptance records, or unrestricted admin access by default.
Pilot Operations Control is a public fake/sample template only. It must not be used as the private pilot system of record.

Real private pilot operations require a separate approved private environment with role access, incident response, official handoff, retention, deletion, and closeout evidence before any real records are collected.
No real meeting links, incident details, official Toastmasters forms, member numbers, payment records, access needs, or private participant states belong in public evidence.
Private Pilot Implementation is a public fake/sample template only; it must not collect or expose real private pilot records.

Real intake requires a separate approved private environment with named operators, minimum fields, role access, privacy notice, consent and withdrawal, incident response, official handoff, retention, deletion, and closeout evidence.
Never store real member numbers, official applications, signatures, payment/card details, meeting links, access needs, incident details, or authority records in the public demo.
Private Pilot Launch Control is a public fake/sample template only; it must not collect or expose real private pilot records.
Real controlled intake cannot open until v2.8 implementation and v2.9 launch gates are approved in a separate private environment.
No real launch meeting links, official Toastmasters records, access needs, incident details, payment details, member numbers, authority records, or private decision records belong in public evidence.

Private Pilot Closeout Decision is a public fake/sample template only; it must not collect or expose real closeout records, evidence, incident details, access needs, official Toastmasters records, payment details, member numbers, or authority records.

No repeat, scale, archive, handoff, or second controlled pilot should proceed until closeout checks, evidence classification, deletion/retention proof, access review, public summary redaction QA, and a decision record are complete.
Public learning from the pilot must be aggregate, redacted, and approved; never-public evidence remains only in the approved private environment.

Private Pilot Authority Approval is a public fake/sample template only; it must not collect or expose real names, emails, access needs, incidents, official Toastmasters records, member numbers, payments, signatures, authority records, or private pilot evidence.

No real private pilot intake, private Decidim workspace, provider handoff, or second-pilot scale path should proceed until authority, Indigenous Data Sovereignty, privacy impact, role access, notice/consent, private environment activation, and scale criteria are approved.
Developer custody and repository maintainership do not equal First Nations community authority.

Activation Drill & Decision Record is a public fake/sample template only; it must not collect or expose real names, emails, access needs, incidents, official Toastmasters records, member numbers, payments, signatures, authority records, access logs, decision records, or private pilot evidence.

No real private pilot intake should proceed until activation drill, signoff matrix, tabletop scenarios, evidence packet, rehearsal tasks, first-72-hour monitoring checks, and recorded go/hold/no-go decision have passed.

Pilot Control Tower is a public fake/sample template only; it must not collect or expose real names, emails, access needs, incidents, official Toastmasters records, member numbers, payments, signatures, authority records, access logs, decision records, or private pilot evidence.
No real private pilot decision should be made from any single page; use the Control Tower to review stage status, blockers, dependencies, evidence classes, owners, and decision route first.

This public v3.4 evidence vault package is fake/sample only. It must not collect or expose real names, emails, access needs, incidents, official Toastmasters records, member numbers, payments, signatures, authority records, access logs, decision records, or private pilot evidence.

Dean Foley / Barayamal may act as advisory and trusted reviewer for this public-safe control package, but does not replace First Nations authority, privacy approval, Indigenous Data Sovereignty approval, official Toastmasters processes, or a private pilot go/no-go decision.

Do not open real intake, activate a private workspace, hand data to a provider, or scale the pilot until the evidence vault, advisory review boundary, authority meeting record, access rules, and decision gates are approved in a separate private environment.

This public v3.5 action docket is fake/sample only. It must not contain real participant names, emails, access needs, incident details, official Toastmasters records, member numbers, payments, signatures, authority records, access logs, private decisions, or private pilot evidence.

Dean Foley / Barayamal may act as advisory and trusted reviewer by raising findings, questions, recommendations, and escalations, but cannot approve the pilot, override First Nations authority, replace privacy or Indigenous Data Sovereignty review, approve official Toastmasters records, or receive unrestricted private evidence.

Every advisory finding must become a public-safe action item with an owner type, evidence needed, acceptance proof, escalation route, and authority decision motion before any real private pilot action proceeds.

Do not open real intake, activate a private workspace, share private evidence, hand data to a provider, or scale the pilot until the advisory action docket is closed or deliberately carried as private meeting conditions by the approved First Nations authority group.

This v3.6 blueprint is public fake/sample configuration only. It must not include real participants, member numbers, contact details, access needs, official Toastmasters records, private authority records, signatures, payments, incident details, or private pilot evidence.

Dean Foley / Barayamal can review this blueprint as initial developer and trusted advisor, but cannot approve First Nations authority, privacy, Indigenous Data Sovereignty, official Toastmasters records, or private participant data access.

Use native Decidim spaces, components, authorizations, admin roles, scheduled tasks, and production checklist controls before custom code. Custom modules are later-stage only after the fake-data blueprint passes acceptance.

Do not build a real private Decidim pilot from this public blueprint until authority, IDS/data governance, privacy, official Toastmasters boundary, private environment, access, support, monitoring, and closeout decisions are approved privately.

This v3.7 two-track onboarding package is public fake/sample data only. Do not collect, upload, seed, screenshot, export, or publish real founder, business owner, mentor, member, guest, official Toastmasters, payment, access, incident, authority, consent, signature, or private support records.

Dean Foley / Barayamal can act as initial developer, custodian, and advisory trusted reviewer for the fake-data onboarding model, but cannot approve First Nations authority, privacy, Indigenous Data Sovereignty, official Toastmasters records, participant eligibility, or private member/founder data access.

Barayamal is the primary First Nations organisation onboarding test for Indigenous founders, business owners, mentors, and program participants. First Nations Toastmasters is a smaller controlled member onboarding tester. Keep the tracks separate, fake-data-only, and gated until private authority, privacy, IDS/data governance, support, official-route, and environment approvals pass.

Do not invite real founders, business owners, Toastmasters members, mentors, guests, or partners into Decidim until a private environment, participant notice, consent route, eligibility path, support route, official Toastmasters boundary, admin revoke test, backup/restore proof, and First Nations authority decision are approved.

This v3.8 lab is public fake/sample data only. Do not create, import, screenshot, export, publish, or test with real founder, business owner, mentor, member, guest, payment, signature, official Toastmasters, authority, consent, support, incident, access, or private pilot records.

Dean Foley / Barayamal may act as initial developer, custodian, advisory reviewer, and trusted reviewer for the fake-data lab, but cannot approve First Nations authority, privacy, Indigenous Data Sovereignty, official Toastmasters records, real participant access, or private pilot launch.

Use the Controlled Sandbox Test Lab only after v3.7 two-track onboarding is understood. Barayamal and First Nations Toastmasters must run as separate fake-data test tracks with separate fake accounts, observer notes, issue queues, evidence folders, go/hold/no-go gates, and closeout decisions.

A real private pilot remains blocked until every required fake-data script passes, critical and high issues are closed or accepted by the private authority process, evidence is redacted, admin revocation is proven, official Toastmasters records remain external, and private authority/privacy/IDS/data governance approvals are recorded outside this public package.

This v3.9 scorecard is public fake/sample data only. Do not record real participant names, contact details, founder/business records, member numbers, payments, signatures, official Toastmasters records, authority minutes, consent evidence, access logs, incident details, support records, or private pilot evidence.

Dean Foley / Barayamal may provide advisory review and initial developer/custodian feedback on the scorecard, but cannot convert fake-data rehearsal results into First Nations authority, privacy, Indigenous Data Sovereignty, official Toastmasters, real participant, or private pilot approval.

Use the Sandbox Rehearsal Scorecard after the v3.8 Controlled Sandbox Test Lab. The scorecard converts fake-data rehearsal results into a decision-ready readout: what passed, what is blocked, what must be remediated, what can be publicly summarized, and what must remain private.

A private pilot can move beyond rehearsal only when blocked critical/high items are closed or formally accepted in a private authority process, role revocation is proven, official Toastmasters records remain external, evidence boundaries are respected, and private authority/privacy/IDS/data governance approvals exist outside this public package.

This v4.0 package is public fake/sample data only. Do not store real participant names, emails, phone numbers, founder or business records, member numbers, payments, signatures, official Toastmasters records, cultural material, support records, access logs, incident details, authority minutes, consent evidence, or private pilot evidence in this public package.

Dean Foley / Barayamal may act as initial developer, custodian, advisory reviewer, and trusted reviewer for this closeout board, but private First Nations authority, privacy, Indigenous Data Sovereignty/data governance, official Toastmasters, safety/support, and real participant decisions remain separate approval responsibilities.

Use the Private Rehearsal Closeout Board after v3.9. It turns the scorecard into a controlled private fake-data execution plan: run scripts, record private evidence status, close blockers, prepare a public-safe update, and choose hold, second rehearsal, no-go, or tightly controlled pilot readiness.

A real participant pilot remains blocked until every critical closeout gate is passed privately, high-risk holds are either closed or formally accepted by the right authority, official Toastmasters records remain external, support pause and admin revocation are proven, and public communications use only the approved public-safe update.

This v4.1 package is public fake/sample data only. Do not collect or publish real names, emails, phone numbers, founder or business details, member numbers, payments, signatures, authority minutes, consent evidence, access logs, support records, incident details, cultural material, official Toastmasters records, or private pilot evidence.

Dean Foley / Barayamal may remain initial developer, custodian, advisory reviewer, and trusted reviewer, but cannot sign First Nations authority, privacy, Indigenous Data Sovereignty/data governance, official Toastmasters, participant consent, support, payment, or real pilot approvals on behalf of the relevant owners.

Use the Pilot Agreement & Organisation Onboarding Pack after the v4.0 closeout board. It converts a private fake-data closeout decision into public-safe joining terms, role commitments, participant notices, data schedules, support protocols, training steps, launch communications, and go/hold/no-go conditions.

First Nations organisations can join only through a private approved pathway after authority, privacy, IDS/data governance, official-route, support, technical, access, retention, and closeout approvals pass. The public pack provides templates and boundaries, not approval itself.

This v4.2 package remains public fake/sample data only. Do not collect or publish real names, emails, phone numbers, business-sensitive details, member numbers, payments, signatures, authority minutes, consent evidence, access logs, support records, incident details, cultural material, official Toastmasters records, private URLs, or private pilot evidence.

Dean Foley / Barayamal may support intake design, trusted review, and developer triage, but cannot approve participant consent, First Nations authority, Indigenous Data Sovereignty/data governance, privacy, official Toastmasters, support, payment, access, or real pilot decisions for other owners.

Use the Private Intake & Consent Triage Pack after the v4.1 agreement pack. It turns public-safe joining templates into a private fake-data intake workflow with field minimisation, consent checks, support and withdrawal routes, access provisioning, approval tickets, communications, risk controls, and launch decisions.

Real private intake can open only after the blocked consent, authority, privacy, IDS/data governance, official-route, support, access, retention, and incident controls pass in private. Until then, use this pack as an intake rehearsal and reviewer checklist only.

This v4.3 package remains public fake/sample data only. Do not publish real names, emails, phone numbers, member numbers, business-sensitive material, payments, signatures, authority minutes, consent evidence, support records, incident details, access logs, official Toastmasters records, cultural material, private URLs, or private pilot evidence.

Dean Foley / Barayamal may act as initial developer, custodian, and trusted reviewer for public-safe and approved fake-data evidence, but cannot approve First Nations authority, privacy, Indigenous Data Sovereignty/data governance, official Toastmasters, support, payment, consent, access, or pilot launch decisions owned by others.

Use the v4.3 Private Approval & Pilot Gate Room after the v4.2 intake triage pack. It converts private intake holds into owner decisions, motions, gate status, risk acceptance, closeout actions, sandbox readiness checks, and public-safe updates.

Recommended decision remains Hold. Run a controlled fake-data private approval meeting first; real participant intake or live Decidim access can open only if authority, privacy, IDS/data governance, official-route, support, access, retention, incident, technical, and closeout gates pass privately.

This v4.4 package remains public fake/sample data only. Private meeting minutes, owner names beyond role labels, authority evidence, consent evidence, member numbers, payments, signatures, support records, incident details, access logs, credentials, backups, exports, official Toastmasters records, business-sensitive details, cultural material, and private URLs must remain outside the public package.

Dean Foley / Barayamal may support docket preparation, developer notes, and trusted-review comments only where an approval owner permits it. Advisory review never replaces First Nations authority, privacy, IDS/data governance, official Toastmasters, support, payment, consent, access, or launch approvals.

Use the v4.4 Private Decision Evidence Docket after the v4.3 gate room. It turns owners, motions, gates, risks, and sandbox checks into a private meeting docket with agenda steps, evidence custody, owner decision records, conflict/recusal checks, redaction, public outcome lines, carry-forward actions, audit log, and communications.

Recommended decision remains Hold. Run the private fake-data decision meeting, close or carry forward every owner decision record, approve redactions, and publish only the public-safe outcome line. Real intake and live Decidim access remain blocked until all private owner approvals pass.

This v4.5 package remains public fake/sample data only. No real names, emails, phone numbers, member numbers, payments, signatures, authority evidence, consent records, support or incident details, access logs, credentials, backups, exports, official Toastmasters records, business-sensitive details, cultural material, private URLs, or live Decidim configuration belong in the public package.

Dean Foley / Barayamal may support implementation planning, developer notes, sample task triage, and trusted review only where the responsible owner permits it. Advisory review never replaces First Nations authority, privacy, IDS/data governance, official Toastmasters, support, payment, consent, access, launch, release lock, rollback, or closeout approvals.

Use the v4.5 Private Implementation Control Ledger after the v4.4 decision docket. It converts owner decisions and carry-forward actions into locked implementation workstreams, dependency checks, build tasks, access controls, acceptance evidence, rollback controls, and public-safe communications.

Recommended decision remains Hold. Keep the implementation release lock closed until every blocked release lock, access control, environment check, change-control item, acceptance test, and rollback control is privately approved. Real intake and live Decidim access remain blocked.

Use v4.6 after the v4.5 Private Implementation Control Ledger. It turns the next steps into a controlled fake/sample pilot execution pack for Barayamal, First Nations Toastmasters, Dean advisory review, and the gated Decidim live-build backlog.

Dean Foley / Barayamal is the initial trusted reviewer and developer/custodian path. Dean may review clarity, technical quality, workflow safety, and sample-data fit, but advisory review never replaces authority, privacy, IDS/data governance, support, official Toastmasters, or community approvals.

Barayamal is the first controlled First Nations organisation sample for Indigenous founders, business owners, mentors, programs, events, and feedback. Use fake/sample records until private owner approvals explicitly permit any real intake.

First Nations Toastmasters is the second controlled tester for guest/member-adjacent workflows. Keep all official member, club, education, payment, and Toastmasters record systems outside Decidim unless the official route privately approves a specific guest-only boundary.

Proceed with private controlled pilot preparation only. Do not open public onboarding, real participant intake, or live Decidim access until all critical v4.5 release locks, v4.6 execution gates, access controls, acceptance tests, rollback paths, and public communications pass privately.

assuranceRoom.safetyRule: No real community records, member data, cultural material, eligibility data, voting data, or real decision outcomes are allowed in the public demo or fake-data sandbox.

seedStudio.safetyRule: No real community records, member lists, cultural material, eligibility data, voting data, youth records, personal service cases, private contact records, or real decision outcomes are allowed.

buildPack.safetyRule: No real community records, member lists, cultural material, eligibility data, voting data, youth records, personal service cases, private contact records, or real decision outcomes are allowed in the public demo or fake-data sandbox.

toastmastersSampleFacts.minimumOfficerSlate: President, one Vice President, and Secretary or Secretary/Treasurer held by three different people
toastmastersSampleFacts.officialMembershipBoundary: Official membership starts only after official Toastmasters paperwork, sponsorship/payment reconciliation, and charter acceptance.

noGoTriggers: public_demo_collects_real_member_or_contact_data
demo_first_or_supporter_contacts_counted_as_charter_members
official_toastmasters_paperwork_stored_in_public_package
payment_or_card_details_collected_in_public_demo
organisation_support_role_implies_authority_over_member_data
sample_initiative_treated_as_real_community_authority
organisationOnboarding.safetyRule: No real membership, address, phone, email, access-need, official application, Toastmasters member-number, payment, signature, private organisation contact, or real authority data belongs in the public demo, public repository, public screenshots, or fake-data Decidim sandbox.
organisationOnboarding.controlledSampleRule: This is not official Toastmasters membership, application, payment, meeting-link, or member-record infrastructure. It is a public fake/sample coordination test only.

noGoTriggers: public_demo_used_as_real_private_intake
real_private_field_appears_in_public_evidence

privatePilotReadiness.safetyRule: Private pilot controls are public templates only. No real membership, address, phone, email, access-need, official application, Toastmasters member-number, payment, signature, private organisation contact, or real authority data belongs in the public demo, public repository, public screenshots, or fake-data Decidim sandbox.
privatePilotReadiness.controlledPilotRule: Private-pilot controls are templates only. Real records require a separate private environment, approved notices, role access, incident response, and closeout.

noGoTriggers: real_private_field_enters_public_demo_or_public_repository
official_toastmasters_record_stored_in_governance_commons_public_package
payment_or_sponsorship_detail_enters_governance_commons
access_need_visible_to_organisation_supporters_or_public_reviewers
withdrawal_or_correction_request_has_no_owner
incident_has_no_response_clock_or_closeout_evidence
public_wording_implies_chartered_club_or_official_membership_before_acceptance
pilot_continues_without_daily_authority_decision
privatePilotOps.safetyRule: Pilot operations controls are public templates only. No real membership, address, phone, email, access-need, official application, Toastmasters member-number, payment, signature, private organisation contact, meeting link, incident detail, or real authority data belongs in the public demo, public repository, public screenshots, or fake-data Decidim sandbox.
privatePilotOps.runDayRule: Operate only after v2.6 readiness approval and keep all real private records in a separate approved private environment with role access, incident response, official handoff, retention, deletion, and closeout evidence.

noGoTriggers: Real private data appears in the public demo, public repository, public screenshots, public CSVs, or fake-data Decidim sandbox.
Official Toastmasters applications, signatures, member numbers, payment/card details, or meeting links are stored in the public package.
The private environment has no named owner, MFA, backup/export, incident route, or deletion rule.
A participant cannot withdraw, ask what is stored, or request correction.
Access needs or incident details are copied into public evidence.
Supporters, mentors, demo-first attendees, or unsure contacts are counted as charter-ready founders without confirmation.

sourceAnchors.label: Maiam nayri Wingara Indigenous Data Sovereignty

privatePilotImplementation.safetyRule: This public package is fake/sample only. Real First Nations Toastmasters pilot records require a separate approved private environment with named operators, privacy controls, official handoff boundaries, incident response, retention, deletion, and closeout evidence.
privatePilotImplementation.implementationRule: Do not open real intake until required implementation actions, required environment controls, never-public field rules, and access register are approved.

noGoTriggers: Any real private field, meeting link, official form, member number, payment detail, access need, incident detail, or authority record appears in the public demo, public repository, public screenshots, public CSVs, or fake-data Decidim sandbox.
Controlled intake opens before privacy notice, consent, withdrawal, access register, private environment smoke test, official handoff boundary, and go/no-go decision are approved.
Supporters, mentors, sponsors, demo-first attendees, or unsure contacts are counted as charter-ready founders without confirmation.
Public wording implies an official Toastmasters club, official membership, charter acceptance, or guaranteed pathway before the official process confirms it.
Incident, access support, withdrawal, deletion, or correction requests have no owner or response clock.
Weekly learning review or closeout decision is skipped, leaving no evidence for proceed, hold, or no-go.

sourceAnchors.label: Maiam nayri Wingara Indigenous Data Sovereignty

privatePilotLaunch.safetyRule: This public v2.9 launch package is fake/sample only. It must not collect or expose real private pilot records, meeting links, official Toastmasters records, access needs, incidents, payment details, member numbers, or authority records.
privatePilotLaunch.launchRule: Do not open controlled real intake until v2.8 implementation gates pass, v2.9 launch gates are approved, and the private environment is the only system of record.
privatePilotLaunch.nextDecision: Proceed, hold, or no-go after first 30 days based on gates, public evidence boundary, official handoff boundary, support, withdrawal, incident, deletion, retention, and learning evidence.

noGoTriggers: Pilot repeats, scales, or hands off without closeout decision evidence.
Private closeout evidence, incident details, access needs, official Toastmasters records, payment details, member numbers, or authority records appear in the public package.
Records inventory, deletion proof, retention reason, access revocation, or withdrawal/correction resolution is missing.
Supporters, sponsors, or public reviewers receive member data, access needs, incidents, official records, or unrestricted private access.
A second pilot opens using old consent, old gates, or old public wording.
Community authority is implied by repository maintainers or developer custody instead of the approved authority pathway.

sourceAnchors.label: Maiam nayri Wingara Indigenous Data Sovereignty

privatePilotCloseout.safetyRule: This public v3.0 closeout package is fake/sample only. It must not collect or expose real closeout records, private pilot evidence, incident details, access needs, official Toastmasters records, payment details, member numbers, or authority records.
privatePilotCloseout.closeoutRule: Do not repeat, scale, archive, or hand off the pilot until closeout checks, evidence ledger, deletion/retention proof, official boundary, and proceed/hold/no-go decision are recorded.

noGoTriggers: Real intake opens before authority and data governance approval are recorded.
Developer custody or repository maintainership is treated as community authority.
Private pilot records enter GitHub Pages, public CSVs, public screenshots, public repo issues, or the fake Decidim sandbox.
Official Toastmasters records, member numbers, signatures, payments, or charter forms are stored in the public package.
Consent, withdrawal, correction, retention, deletion, or incident response is missing or untested.
A provider or public reviewer receives real participant data before contract, authority, and privacy approval.
Scale or second pilot proceeds without closeout, redaction QA, and authority record.

sourceAnchors.label: Maiam nayri Wingara Indigenous Data Sovereignty
NHMRC Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander research ethics

privatePilotAuthority.safetyRule: This public v3.1 authority package is fake/sample only. It must not collect or expose real names, emails, access needs, incidents, official Toastmasters records, member numbers, payments, signatures, authority records, or private pilot evidence.
privatePilotAuthority.approvalRule: Do not activate any real private pilot, private Decidim workspace, EOI intake, provider handoff, or second pilot until authority, Indigenous Data Sovereignty, privacy impact, role access, participant notice, private environment, and scale criteria are approved.

noGoTriggers: Real intake opens before the activation drill, tabletop scenarios, and signoff matrix are completed.
Authority decision is recorded without quorum, delegation, conditions, expiry, or stop triggers.
Private pilot records, access logs, incident evidence, official Toastmasters records, payment details, member numbers, or support details appear in public files.
No-private-access roles, providers, public reviewers, or supporters can view private records.
Withdrawal, correction, support, incident pause, breach assessment, backup/restore, deletion, or public redaction QA fails.
Official Toastmasters forms, signatures, payments, member numbers, or charter records enter the public package or private pilot tool.
Activation continues past the first 72 hours without authority checkpoint, monitoring evidence, or closeout trigger.

privatePilotActivationDrill.safetyRule: This public v3.2 activation drill package is fake/sample only. It must not collect or expose real names, emails, access needs, incidents, official Toastmasters records, member numbers, payments, signatures, authority records, access logs, decision records, or private pilot evidence.
privatePilotActivationDrill.activationRule: Do not open real private pilot intake until the activation drill, signoff matrix, tabletop scenarios, evidence packet, rehearsal tasks, first-72-hour monitoring checks, and recorded go/hold/no-go decision have passed.

noGoTriggers: NG-001: Any real name, email, phone, address, access need, incident detail, payment, member number, signature, authority record, or official Toastmasters record in public files
NG-002: No named First Nations authority or quorum for activation decision
NG-003: Data classes, purpose, access, retention, deletion, or reporting not approved
NG-004: Notice, consent, withdrawal, correction, PIA, retention, or incident response is incomplete
NG-005: No approved workspace, access register, MFA, backup, export, delete, restore, or logs
NG-006: No-private-access roles, provider, supporter, or public reviewer can see private records
NG-007: Forms, member numbers, signatures, payments, or charter records enter public/pilot package
NG-008: No owner can pause intake, support participant, or run breach/cultural safety escalation
NG-009: Participant cannot withdraw or correct private record
NG-010: Provider gets real export before authority, contract, privacy, and access pass
NG-015: Second pilot, provider handoff, or broader rollout starts before closeout and authority record
NG-016: Go/hold/no-go record lacks conditions, expiry, owner, no-go triggers, or monitoring schedule
NG-017: No hour 0-72 checks or authority checkpoint after activation

sourceAnchors.label: Maiam nayri Wingara Indigenous Data Sovereignty

pilotControlTower.safetyRule: This public v3.3 Control Tower is fake/sample only. It must not collect or expose real names, emails, access needs, incidents, official Toastmasters records, member numbers, payments, signatures, authority records, access logs, decision records, or private pilot evidence.
pilotControlTower.controlRule: Do not make a real activation, provider handoff, or scale decision from any single page. Use the Control Tower to review stage status, blockers, dependencies, evidence classes, owners, and decision route first.

noGoTriggers: Dean or Barayamal advisory review is treated as final First Nations authority.
Real evidence is uploaded to the public static demo, public repository, public screenshots, public CSVs, or fake-data Decidim sandbox.
Authority quorum, IDS/data governance, privacy, role access, or official Toastmasters boundary is missing.
Reviewer notes include real names, contact details, access needs, incidents, official records, payments, member numbers, signatures, or private decisions.
A provider or public reviewer receives real private evidence before contract, authority, privacy, and access approval.
The authority meeting records a go decision without conditions, expiry, no-go triggers, monitoring, support, and closeout owner.

sourceAnchors.label: Maiam nayri Wingara Indigenous Data Sovereignty
NHMRC Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander research ethics

privatePilotEvidenceVault.safetyRule: This public v3.4 evidence vault package is fake/sample only. It must not collect or expose real names, emails, access needs, incidents, official Toastmasters records, member numbers, payments, signatures, authority records, access logs, decision records, or private pilot evidence.
privatePilotEvidenceVault.advisoryRule: Dean Foley / Barayamal may act as advisory and trusted reviewer for this public-safe control package, but does not replace First Nations authority, privacy approval, Indigenous Data Sovereignty approval, official Toastmasters processes, or a private pilot go/no-go decision.
privatePilotEvidenceVault.approvalRule: Do not open real intake, activate a private workspace, hand data to a provider, or scale the pilot until the evidence vault, advisory review boundary, authority meeting record, access rules, and decision gates are approved in a separate private environment.

noGoTriggers: Dean or Barayamal advisory review is treated as final approval.
A blocked action item is left without an owner, evidence request, escalation route, or meeting motion.
Real evidence, names, access needs, incidents, official Toastmasters records, payments, member numbers, signatures, access logs, or private decisions appear in public files.
Authority quorum, Indigenous Data Sovereignty, privacy, private environment, support, incident, or official Toastmasters boundary decisions are missing.
Provider, public reviewer, or advisory reviewer receives unrestricted private evidence before access approval.
The meeting records go without closing blocked motions or explicitly carrying conditions, expiry, monitoring, support, stop triggers, and closeout owner.

sourceAnchors.label: Maiam nayri Wingara Indigenous Data Sovereignty
NHMRC Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander research ethics

advisoryActionDocket.safetyRule: This public v3.5 action docket is fake/sample only. It must not contain real participant names, emails, access needs, incident details, official Toastmasters records, member numbers, payments, signatures, authority records, access logs, private decisions, or private pilot evidence.
advisoryActionDocket.advisoryRule: Dean Foley / Barayamal may act as advisory and trusted reviewer by raising findings, questions, recommendations, and escalations, but cannot approve the pilot, override First Nations authority, replace privacy or Indigenous Data Sovereignty review, approve official Toastmasters records, or receive unrestricted private evidence.
advisoryActionDocket.docketRule: Every advisory finding must become a public-safe action item with an owner type, evidence needed, acceptance proof, escalation route, and authority decision motion before any real private pilot action proceeds.
advisoryActionDocket.approvalRule: Do not open real intake, activate a private workspace, share private evidence, hand data to a provider, or scale the pilot until the advisory action docket is closed or deliberately carried as private meeting conditions by the approved First Nations authority group.

noGoTriggers: A builder treats the public blueprint as authority to open real intake.
Real participant, member, official Toastmasters, access, payment, incident, or authority data appears in the public Decidim sandbox, repository, screenshots, CSVs, or JSON.
A custom Decidim module is proposed before native spaces, components, authorizations, and permissions are tested.
Dean or Barayamal is given unrestricted private records because of the advisory/developer role.
The private pilot build starts without a target Decidim version, environment owner, backup/restore proof, SMTP/job setup, admin revoke test, and redaction QA.
Official Toastmasters records, forms, payments, member numbers, or charter acceptance are mixed into the public governance commons demo.
decidimBuildBlueprint.safetyRule: This v3.6 blueprint is public fake/sample configuration only. It must not include real participants, member numbers, contact details, access needs, official Toastmasters records, private authority records, signatures, payments, incident details, or private pilot evidence.
decidimBuildBlueprint.advisoryRule: Dean Foley / Barayamal can review this blueprint as initial developer and trusted advisor, but cannot approve First Nations authority, privacy, Indigenous Data Sovereignty, official Toastmasters records, or private participant data access.
decidimBuildBlueprint.buildRule: Use native Decidim spaces, components, authorizations, admin roles, scheduled tasks, and production checklist controls before custom code. Custom modules are later-stage only after the fake-data blueprint passes acceptance.
decidimBuildBlueprint.approvalRule: Do not build a real private Decidim pilot from this public blueprint until authority, IDS/data governance, privacy, official Toastmasters boundary, private environment, access, support, monitoring, and closeout decisions are approved privately.

twoTrackPilotOnboarding.operatingRule: Barayamal is the primary First Nations organisation onboarding test for Indigenous founders, business owners, mentors, and program participants. First Nations Toastmasters is a smaller controlled member onboarding tester. Keep the tracks separate, fake-data-only, and gated until private authority, privacy, IDS/data governance, support, official-route, and environment approvals pass.
twoTrackPilotOnboarding.advisoryRule: Dean Foley / Barayamal can act as initial developer, custodian, and advisory trusted reviewer for the fake-data onboarding model, but cannot approve First Nations authority, privacy, Indigenous Data Sovereignty, official Toastmasters records, participant eligibility, or private member/founder data access.
twoTrackPilotOnboarding.approvalRule: Do not invite real founders, business owners, Toastmasters members, mentors, guests, or partners into Decidim until a private environment, participant notice, consent route, eligibility path, support route, official Toastmasters boundary, admin revoke test, backup/restore proof, and First Nations authority decision are approved.
twoTrackPilotOnboarding.safetyRule: This v3.7 two-track onboarding package is public fake/sample data only. Do not collect, upload, seed, screenshot, export, or publish real founder, business owner, mentor, member, guest, official Toastmasters, payment, access, incident, authority, consent, signature, or private support records.

controlledSandboxTestLab.operatingRule: Use the Controlled Sandbox Test Lab only after v3.7 two-track onboarding is understood. Barayamal and First Nations Toastmasters must run as separate fake-data test tracks with separate fake accounts, observer notes, issue queues, evidence folders, go/hold/no-go gates, and closeout decisions.
controlledSandboxTestLab.advisoryRule: Dean Foley / Barayamal may act as initial developer, custodian, advisory reviewer, and trusted reviewer for the fake-data lab, but cannot approve First Nations authority, privacy, Indigenous Data Sovereignty, official Toastmasters records, real participant access, or private pilot launch.
controlledSandboxTestLab.decisionRule: A real private pilot remains blocked until every required fake-data script passes, critical and high issues are closed or accepted by the private authority process, evidence is redacted, admin revocation is proven, official Toastmasters records remain external, and private authority/privacy/IDS/data governance approvals are recorded outside this public package.
controlledSandboxTestLab.safetyRule: This v3.8 lab is public fake/sample data only. Do not create, import, screenshot, export, publish, or test with real founder, business owner, mentor, member, guest, payment, signature, official Toastmasters, authority, consent, support, incident, access, or private pilot records.

sandboxRehearsalScorecard.operatingRule: Use the Sandbox Rehearsal Scorecard after the v3.8 Controlled Sandbox Test Lab. The scorecard converts fake-data rehearsal results into a decision-ready readout: what passed, what is blocked, what must be remediated, what can be publicly summarized, and what must remain private.
sandboxRehearsalScorecard.advisoryRule: Dean Foley / Barayamal may provide advisory review and initial developer/custodian feedback on the scorecard, but cannot convert fake-data rehearsal results into First Nations authority, privacy, Indigenous Data Sovereignty, official Toastmasters, real participant, or private pilot approval.
sandboxRehearsalScorecard.decisionRule: A private pilot can move beyond rehearsal only when blocked critical/high items are closed or formally accepted in a private authority process, role revocation is proven, official Toastmasters records remain external, evidence boundaries are respected, and private authority/privacy/IDS/data governance approvals exist outside this public package.
sandboxRehearsalScorecard.safetyRule: This v3.9 scorecard is public fake/sample data only. Do not record real participant names, contact details, founder/business records, member numbers, payments, signatures, official Toastmasters records, authority minutes, consent evidence, access logs, incident details, support records, or private pilot evidence.

privateRehearsalCloseout.operatingRule: Use the Private Rehearsal Closeout Board after v3.9. It turns the scorecard into a controlled private fake-data execution plan: run scripts, record private evidence status, close blockers, prepare a public-safe update, and choose hold, second rehearsal, no-go, or tightly controlled pilot readiness.
privateRehearsalCloseout.advisoryRule: Dean Foley / Barayamal may act as initial developer, custodian, advisory reviewer, and trusted reviewer for this closeout board, but private First Nations authority, privacy, Indigenous Data Sovereignty/data governance, official Toastmasters, safety/support, and real participant decisions remain separate approval responsibilities.
privateRehearsalCloseout.safetyRule: This v4.0 package is public fake/sample data only. Do not store real participant names, emails, phone numbers, founder or business records, member numbers, payments, signatures, official Toastmasters records, cultural material, support records, access logs, incident details, authority minutes, consent evidence, or private pilot evidence in this public package.
privateRehearsalCloseout.launchRule: A real participant pilot remains blocked until every critical closeout gate is passed privately, high-risk holds are either closed or formally accepted by the right authority, official Toastmasters records remain external, support pause and admin revocation are proven, and public communications use only the approved public-safe update.

pilotAgreementOnboarding.operatingRule: Use the Pilot Agreement & Organisation Onboarding Pack after the v4.0 closeout board. It converts a private fake-data closeout decision into public-safe joining terms, role commitments, participant notices, data schedules, support protocols, training steps, launch communications, and go/hold/no-go conditions.
pilotAgreementOnboarding.advisoryRule: Dean Foley / Barayamal may remain initial developer, custodian, advisory reviewer, and trusted reviewer, but cannot sign First Nations authority, privacy, Indigenous Data Sovereignty/data governance, official Toastmasters, participant consent, support, payment, or real pilot approvals on behalf of the relevant owners.
pilotAgreementOnboarding.safetyRule: This v4.1 package is public fake/sample data only. Do not collect or publish real names, emails, phone numbers, founder or business details, member numbers, payments, signatures, authority minutes, consent evidence, access logs, support records, incident details, cultural material, official Toastmasters records, or private pilot evidence.
pilotAgreementOnboarding.launchRule: First Nations organisations can join only through a private approved pathway after authority, privacy, IDS/data governance, official-route, support, technical, access, retention, and closeout approvals pass. The public pack provides templates and boundaries, not approval itself.

privateIntakeConsentTriage.operatingRule: Use the Private Intake & Consent Triage Pack after the v4.1 agreement pack. It turns public-safe joining templates into a private fake-data intake workflow with field minimisation, consent checks, support and withdrawal routes, access provisioning, approval tickets, communications, risk controls, and launch decisions.
privateIntakeConsentTriage.advisoryRule: Dean Foley / Barayamal may support intake design, trusted review, and developer triage, but cannot approve participant consent, First Nations authority, Indigenous Data Sovereignty/data governance, privacy, official Toastmasters, support, payment, access, or real pilot decisions for other owners.
privateIntakeConsentTriage.safetyRule: This v4.2 package remains public fake/sample data only. Do not collect or publish real names, emails, phone numbers, business-sensitive details, member numbers, payments, signatures, authority minutes, consent evidence, access logs, support records, incident details, cultural material, official Toastmasters records, private URLs, or private pilot evidence.
privateIntakeConsentTriage.launchRule: Real private intake can open only after the blocked consent, authority, privacy, IDS/data governance, official-route, support, access, retention, and incident controls pass in private. Until then, use this pack as an intake rehearsal and reviewer checklist only.

privateApprovalPilotGate.operatingRule: Use the v4.3 Private Approval & Pilot Gate Room after the v4.2 intake triage pack. It converts private intake holds into owner decisions, motions, gate status, risk acceptance, closeout actions, sandbox readiness checks, and public-safe updates.
privateApprovalPilotGate.advisoryRule: Dean Foley / Barayamal may act as initial developer, custodian, and trusted reviewer for public-safe and approved fake-data evidence, but cannot approve First Nations authority, privacy, Indigenous Data Sovereignty/data governance, official Toastmasters, support, payment, consent, access, or pilot launch decisions owned by others.
privateApprovalPilotGate.safetyRule: This v4.3 package remains public fake/sample data only. Do not publish real names, emails, phone numbers, member numbers, business-sensitive material, payments, signatures, authority minutes, consent evidence, support records, incident details, access logs, official Toastmasters records, cultural material, private URLs, or private pilot evidence.
privateApprovalPilotGate.decisionRule: Recommended decision remains Hold. Run a controlled fake-data private approval meeting first; real participant intake or live Decidim access can open only if authority, privacy, IDS/data governance, official-route, support, access, retention, incident, technical, and closeout gates pass privately.

sourceAnchors.label: NHMRC Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander research ethics

privateDecisionEvidenceDocket.operatingRule: Use the v4.4 Private Decision Evidence Docket after the v4.3 gate room. It turns owners, motions, gates, risks, and sandbox checks into a private meeting docket with agenda steps, evidence custody, owner decision records, conflict/recusal checks, redaction, public outcome lines, carry-forward actions, audit log, and communications.
privateDecisionEvidenceDocket.advisoryRule: Dean Foley / Barayamal may support docket preparation, developer notes, and trusted-review comments only where an approval owner permits it. Advisory review never replaces First Nations authority, privacy, IDS/data governance, official Toastmasters, support, payment, consent, access, or launch approvals.
privateDecisionEvidenceDocket.safetyRule: This v4.4 package remains public fake/sample data only. Private meeting minutes, owner names beyond role labels, authority evidence, consent evidence, member numbers, payments, signatures, support records, incident details, access logs, credentials, backups, exports, official Toastmasters records, business-sensitive details, cultural material, and private URLs must remain outside the public package.
privateDecisionEvidenceDocket.decisionRule: Recommended decision remains Hold. Run the private fake-data decision meeting, close or carry forward every owner decision record, approve redactions, and publish only the public-safe outcome line. Real intake and live Decidim access remain blocked until all private owner approvals pass.

privateImplementationControlLedger.operatingRule: Use the v4.5 Private Implementation Control Ledger after the v4.4 decision docket. It converts owner decisions and carry-forward actions into locked implementation workstreams, dependency checks, build tasks, access controls, acceptance evidence, rollback controls, and public-safe communications.
privateImplementationControlLedger.advisoryRule: Dean Foley / Barayamal may support implementation planning, developer notes, sample task triage, and trusted review only where the responsible owner permits it. Advisory review never replaces First Nations authority, privacy, IDS/data governance, official Toastmasters, support, payment, consent, access, launch, release lock, rollback, or closeout approvals.
privateImplementationControlLedger.safetyRule: This v4.5 package remains public fake/sample data only. No real names, emails, phone numbers, member numbers, payments, signatures, authority evidence, consent records, support or incident details, access logs, credentials, backups, exports, official Toastmasters records, business-sensitive details, cultural material, private URLs, or live Decidim configuration belong in the public package.
privateImplementationControlLedger.decisionRule: Recommended decision remains Hold. Keep the implementation release lock closed until every blocked release lock, access control, environment check, change-control item, acceptance test, and rollback control is privately approved. Real intake and live Decidim access remain blocked.

controlledPilotExecution.executionRule: Use v4.6 after the v4.5 Private Implementation Control Ledger. It turns the next steps into a controlled fake/sample pilot execution pack for Barayamal, First Nations Toastmasters, Dean advisory review, and the gated Decidim live-build backlog.
controlledPilotExecution.deanBoundaryRule: Dean Foley / Barayamal is the initial trusted reviewer and developer/custodian path. Dean may review clarity, technical quality, workflow safety, and sample-data fit, but advisory review never replaces authority, privacy, IDS/data governance, support, official Toastmasters, or community approvals.
controlledPilotExecution.barayamalRule: Barayamal is the first controlled First Nations organisation sample for Indigenous founders, business owners, mentors, programs, events, and feedback. Use fake/sample records until private owner approvals explicitly permit any real intake.
controlledPilotExecution.toastmastersRule: First Nations Toastmasters is the second controlled tester for guest/member-adjacent workflows. Keep all official member, club, education, payment, and Toastmasters record systems outside Decidim unless the official route privately approves a specific guest-only boundary.
controlledPilotExecution.liveLaunchRule: Proceed with private controlled pilot preparation only. Do not open public onboarding, real participant intake, or live Decidim access until all critical v4.5 release locks, v4.6 execution gates, access controls, acceptance tests, rollback paths, and public communications pass privately.
